notes:
Ốm usually means sick / ill (not healthy). It describes a person’s health state.

It’s an adjective, so you often use it with bị or after verbs like cảm thấy, trông.  

Ốm can also mean thin / skinny (about body shape). For body size, it’s close to gầy; opposite of mập / béo.
exampleSentences: Hôm qua em không đi học vì em bị ốm.
Anh ấy thường uống nhiều nước khi cảm thấy ốm.
Chị nhớ mặc ấm kẻo bị ốm trong mùa đông nhé.

example.vi: Tôi cảm thấy ốm hôm nay.

definitions.vi: Trong tình trạng sức khỏe kém; ốm.

exampleSentencesTranslated: Yesterday I didn't go to school because I was sick.
He usually drinks a lot of water when he feels sick.
Remember to dress warmly or you will get sick in winter.
notes_md:
**Ốm** usually means **sick / ill** (not healthy). It describes a person’s health state.

It’s an adjective, so you often use it with **bị** or after verbs like **cảm thấy, trông**.  

**Ốm** can also mean **thin / skinny** (about body shape). For body size, it’s close to **gầy**; opposite of **mập / béo**.
